title: Frameless Nightstands — a Matching Pair from One Sheet of Plywood
summary: Two frameless plywood nightstands cut from a single sheet — no face frame at all, base and cove moulding doing the trim work instead, with a drawer per nightstand.
description:
This is a genuinely efficient design: rip one 3/4" sheet of plywood into 15-3/4"-wide strips and both nightstand carcasses come from it, with a second sheet of 1/4" ply covering both backs and both drawer bottoms. Unlike a face-framed nightstand, there's no separate solid-wood frame at all — the plywood edges themselves are the finished surface, dressed up entirely with base moulding wrapped around the bottom and cove moulding along the top edge rather than a frame hiding the plywood underneath.

A single 1x2 shelf support board does three jobs at once: it ties the bottom of the carcass together, keeps the whole piece square, and gives the base moulding something solid to nail into — a good example of one part doing structural, dimensional, and finish work simultaneously rather than needing three separate pieces.

Install the drawer no more than 7" from the top, which is what lets the drawer face fully hide the plywood drawer box behind it. Attach the drawer face with a couple of finish nails from the outside first, test the fit, then remove the drawer and back up the face with real screws driven from inside — nail first, screw second, is what lets you make small positioning adjustments before committing to permanent fasteners.
timeLabel: 5–7 hrs for the pair, plus finish

tools.note: Mitering the base and cove moulding corners.

steps.title: Build the basic boxes
steps.body: Build the basic box for each nightstand out of the ripped plywood. If staining, edge-band the front edges before assembly. The shelf can be fixed now, or made floating with shelf pins and installed after the drawer.

steps.title: Add the shelf support
steps.body: Add the 1x2 bottom shelf support — it ties the bottom together, keeps the piece square, and gives you something solid to attach the base moulding to.

steps.title: Attach the backs
steps.body: Attach the plywood backs with glue and finish nails.

steps.title: Add the base moulding
steps.body: Miter the corners of the base moulding and wrap it around the bottom with finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes
steps.body: Build each drawer box with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, and attach the 1/4" plywood bottom with 3/4" brad nails and glue — use the bottom panel to help square up the box.

steps.title: Install the drawers
steps.body: Install each drawer inside its cabinet no more than 7" from the top — this is what lets the drawer face fully hide the drawer box — inset 3/4" from the outside of the nightstand, using the drawer slides.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces
steps.body: Attach a knob or handle to each drawer face first, then attach the face to the front of its drawer with a couple of finish nails from the outside. Remove the drawer and add a few 1-1/4" screws from inside for real holding power.

steps.title: Trim the drawer bottom and finish
steps.body: Trim the bottom edge of each drawer opening with the remaining 1x2, attached with pocket holes or 1-1/4" finish nails. Add the cove moulding to the top edges of both nightstands. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Pair of frameless plywood nightstands built from one sheet
